                                                                CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                          In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                          Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                   Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   June   July     Aug.   Oct.    Nov.   Dec.

                     Household data
                   Annual averages                                   X
                   Revised seasonally adjusted series                       X

                    Seasonally adjusted data

                    Persons of Spanish origin
                    Vietnam-Era veterans and nonveterans

                   National annual averages:

                         The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The October 1975 issue marks
                  the introduction of March 1974 benchmarks.

                    The formula for estimating the weekly earned income
            credit (Q) is:                                                                  where Y = gross annual earnings

                             Q= 1/52 [$400- 0.1 (52X-$4,000)]                               Then, substituting,

                             Q= $15.38 - 0.1 X                                                     0.15 (Y - $1,000     $5,100) + $140= ($800- 0.1 Y)
                                                                                                                                                 + $140
                   where

                             X = gross average weekly earnings                              Solving this equation yields

                                                                                                   Y = $6,860
                   The formula for estimating the taxable income weekly
            credit (K) is:                                                                  The point at which income tax owed equals tax credits
                                                                                      may be defined as follows (note there is no Q in this case):
                             K = ( n - $35)/52
                                                                                                   T = 52K
                   where
                                                                                            and    T = t(Y - X      - E) + C
                             n = number of exemptions
                  Adding the two new factors, Q and K, yields the following                 where T, t, Y, X , E, C, and K are defined in the same

                   For full name of labor area and definition of area, see Area Trends in Employment and Unemployment published by the Employment and Training Administration.

                            New seasonal adjustment procedures for the Consumer Price Index were introduced with the release of the January 1976 CPI. Consequently,
                          the seasonally adjusted CPI has been revised back to January 1967. The series shown below which use the seasonally adjusted CPI in their com-
                          putation have been revised accordingly. The weekly earnings figures (in 1967 dollars) appear currently in table C-8.  They are also published
                          along with the hourly earnings figures (in 1967 dollars) in the Real Earnings press release.
